oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Bringing added value to our dairy producers through records analysis
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: (Buckham) Sporer, Kelly Rebecca
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: records analysis
key metrics
reproduction
death rate
heifer non-completetion rate
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Basic skills and understanding of dairy producer records and how to analyze them bring value to producers by monitoring key metrics on the operation. The goal should be to maximize producer profitability with customized insights that can drive management decisions as no two dairies are the same. Changes in weather, protocols, nutrition, grouping, etc. necessitate mon­itoring of results. Key outputs are: milk and component produc­tion, reproduction, udder health and milk quality, death and cull rates, and heifer health and survival. Benchmarks from the industry are widely available, but individual farm goals should be established while focusing on progress over perfection. “Problems” should be reframed as “challenges” or “opportuni­ties”. Recordkeeping is an ongoing challenge at many dairies, and poor or incomplete data lead to poor or incomplete analy­sis. Countless records management software systems are avail­able and do not necessarily “talk” to one another effectively, leading to challenges. The aim of this presentation is to cover key metrics and benchmarks, discuss challenges in records analysis, and demonstrate basic skills while generating produc­tive discussion and providing valuable take-home insights for recent veterinary graduates.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 41-44
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
